steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says that neither History nor any works predating it mention the Round Table, yet Le Morte d'Arthur does. The blank must explain why Malory included something none of his known sources contain.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Le Morte d'Arthur is more historically accurate than History because the Mabinogion had not been written when Geoffrey of Monmouth was writing his work.': The passage never discusses historical accuracy, and the Mabinogion's timeline is irrelevant to why the Round Table appears. ✗
  • Choice B — 'when a version of an Arthurian legend contradicted the version in History, Malory preferred to include Geoffrey of Monmouth's version in Le Morte d'Arthur.': This claims Malory deferred to Geoffrey — but Geoffrey's History doesn't mention the Round Table at all, so deferring to Geoffrey can't explain its presence. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Malory encountered the Round Table in a source that Geoffrey of Monmouth was not familiar with when writing his History.': If Malory used a source Geoffrey didn't know, that directly explains why the Round Table appears in Le Morte d'Arthur but not in History or works that predate it. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Geoffrey of Monmouth's accounts of Arthurian legends in History are more similar overall in content to the accounts in the Mabinogion than they are to the accounts in Le Morte d'Arthur.': A similarity comparison between sources doesn't address why the Round Table is absent from earlier works but present in Malory's. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
